summary: A low, symmetrical single-storey complex with a central raised tower and surrounding stepped parapet walls. The layout is a roughly rectangular courtyard with a T-shaped paved walkway leading to a square elevated central platform/tower. Two mirrored long wing blocks sit either side of the walkway; decorative vertical corner pillars and low outer boundary blocks define the site.

shape.overall_form: rectangular courtyard with central raised tower and two mirrored side wings connected by a paved T-shaped path
shape.symmetry: approximate bilateral symmetry along the walkway axis

materials.name: quartz
materials.role: white corner pillars, parapet and edging

materials.name: glass
materials.role: uncertain - small window/air gaps maybe present

materials.name: glowstone
materials.role: light sources on parapet and tower

elements: central tower/platform with flat layered roof
two mirrored elongated wings with stepped horizontal bands
T-shaped brick walkway leading to central platform
low outer boundary of spaced white blocks
glowstone light markers on parapet and tower
rebuild_hints: Start with a rectangular base footprint; use a darker stone (stonebrick) base 1-2 blocks high around the perimeter.

Build two identical elongated wing blocks on either side of a central path; each wing has stacked horizontal material bands: stonebrick base, wood band, sandstone band, then quartz parapet with a thin brick trim near the top.

Create a T-shaped brick-paved walkway centered in the rectangle leading to a raised square platform. Raise a narrow central tower/pillar up from the platform and cap it with a flat multi-layered roof (brick center, quartz edge).
Place vertical quartz corner pillars at ends of wings that extend above the parapet by several blocks.
Add spaced single-block white markers around the outer edge to match the border and glowstone blocks on top of parapets and the tower for lighting.
uncertainties: Exact block types for the yellow/tan middle band: appears to be sandstone but could be another light stone (confidence 0.85).
Lower mid-tone horizontal band looks wooden (oak planks) but could be stripped logs or another wood type (confidence 0.6).
Some small details (possible windows or recesses) are hard to confirm from exterior angles; interior layout is unknown.
Exact overall footprint dimensions are estimated from aerial view; real block counts may differ by a few blocks.
